seoTitle: Make Google Docs Read Aloud to You (Step by Step)
metaDescription: Set up Google Docs to read your documents aloud with accessibility settings, browser read-aloud tools, and mobile screen reader options.
intro: Listening back to your writing is one of the fastest ways to catch awkward phrasing or typos. This walkthrough shows how to switch on Google Docs' read-aloud capabilities, from the document's own accessibility settings to browser tools and mobile screen readers, so you can review your files hands-free.

steps.heading: Enable accessibility screen reader support
steps.body: Open the accessibility menu in your document settings to turn on native screen reader compatibility.

steps.heading: Trigger read-aloud with keyboard shortcuts
steps.body: Use standard keyboard shortcuts to start reading a selected paragraph or the whole page instantly.

steps.heading: Use your browser's built-in reading tools
steps.body: Microsoft Edge's Read Aloud or Chrome's Reading Mode can read your document text fluidly without any Docs-specific setup.

steps.heading: Customize playback
steps.body: Adjust reading speed, pause tracks, and switch between voice profiles from the text-to-speech controls.

steps.heading: Read on mobile
steps.body: Use Select to Speak or Spoken Content in the Google Docs app on Android or iOS to listen on the go.

faq.question: Is there a built-in way to have Google Docs read text aloud?
faq.answer: Yes, enabling accessibility screen reader support in your document settings activates native read-aloud functionality.

faq.question: Can I skip complex tables when listening?
faq.answer: You may need to manually skip complex layout tables since they can sometimes cause audio rendering issues.

faq.question: Which browsers support read-aloud for Google Docs?
faq.answer: Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome both have native reading tools that work well with documents opened in the browser.

faq.question: Can I adjust how fast the voice reads?
faq.answer: Yes, the playback controls let you change reading speed and switch voice accents to suit your preference.
